    difficult to value. Industry-standard discount rates in technical reports may not adequately reflect the risk profile of pre-revenue mining ventures. The NAV tracking engine applies stage-appropriate risk pricing, but there is no guarantee that any valuation methodology will prove accurate.

  • Cycle Timing Risk. Mining is deeply cyclical. Vault investments

    made near the peak of a commodity or equity cycle may experience extended periods of depressed valuations before recovery. The timing of cycle turns is inherently unpredictable, and exploration companies are disproportionately affected because their access to capital depends heavily on market sentiment.

  • Regulatory Risk. The regulatory landscape for both tokenised

    securities and mining operations is evolving. Changes in applicable laws could impact platform operations, token transferability, or investor access. Vault tokens may be classified differently across jurisdictions.

  • Smart Contract Risk. Despite independent audits, smart contracts

    may contain vulnerabilities. Exploits or bugs could result in partial or total loss of funds.

  • Liquidity Risk. While the AMM provides secondary market

    liquidity, there is no guarantee of sufficient depth or favourable pricing. In stressed market conditions, liquidity may decline significantly. The liquidity premium described in this whitepaper is a structural expectation, not a guarantee.

  • Counterparty Risk. The protocol depends on the operational

    integrity of the Bermuda Top Co custodian, SAC administrators, and underlying mining companies.

  • Technology Risk. Platform outages, blockchain network

    congestion, oracle failures, or data feed interruptions could disrupt operations and delay distributions.

← Team & GovernanceConclusion →
Minestarters · Whitepaper v1.0 · March 2026 · For informational purposes only
Conclusion

13. Conclusion

The mining exploration sector is structurally broken. The companies responsible for discovering the mineral deposits that the energy transition requires are financed through a model that penalises them at every turn through illiquidity discounts, predatory terms, dilutive fundraising, and near-zero secondary market liquidity. The investors who might provide that capital face concentration risk, opacity, compliance friction, and entry barriers that exclude all but a narrow, geographically concentrated pool of repeat participants. The result is a sector starved of investment at the precise moment global demand requires it to scale.

Minestarters was built to close that gap. The vault model packages exploration-stage exposure into curated, diversified portfolios held within a Bermuda SAC custody framework, with cash flows routed through Revenue Participation Agreements, valuations tracked on-chain through a purpose-built NAV engine, and secondary market liquidity delivered through Asset-Referenced Tokens and a permissioned AMM. The structure preserves the discovery torque that makes exploration compelling while eliminating the concentration risk, illiquidity, and compliance friction that have suppressed institutional allocation for decades.

The platform is not theoretical. The corporate structure is in progress. The curator programme is active through three established networks. The project pipeline includes 15 projects across five commodities and seven countries with a combined capital value exceeding \$3 billion. The team combines decades of hands-on exploration experience, proven Web3 capital raising capability, and deep smart contract engineering expertise.

The opportunity is to build the infrastructure layer for tokenised mining exploration investment at the moment when the market conditions, the technology infrastructure, and the capital demand are simultaneously aligned for the first time.

APPENDIX / Disclaimer

14. Disclaimer

THIS DOCUMENT IS NOT AN OFFER OR SOLICITATION

This whitepaper is provided for informational and discussion purposes only. It does not constitute, and should not be construed as, an offer to sell, a solicitation of an offer to buy, or a recommendation for any security, token, digital asset, investment product, or service in any jurisdiction. No part of this document should be considered as investment, financial, legal, regulatory, accounting, or tax advice. Prospective participants should consult their own independent professional advisors before taking any action based on information contained herein.

NO REPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES

The information in this whitepaper is provided on an \"as is\" basis without any representations, warranties, or guarantees of any kind, whether express, implied, or statutory. Minestarters, its affiliates, directors, officers, employees, advisors, and agents (collectively, the \"Team\") make no representation or warranty as to the accuracy, completeness, reliability, timeliness, or suitability of the information contained herein. The Team expressly disclaims any and all liability for any loss or damage arising from reliance on this document.

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

This whitepaper contains forward-looking statements, projections, estimates, and illustrations relating to the future performance and development of the Minestarters protocol, its technology, tokenomics, and market opportunity. These statements are based on current expectations and assumptions as of the date of publication and involve known and unknown risks, uncertainties, and other factors that may cause actual results, performance, or achievements to differ materially from those expressed or implied. Words such as \"expects,\" \"anticipates,\" \"targets,\" \"aims,\" \"plans,\" \"intends,\" \"believes,\" \"estimates,\" \"may,\" \"will,\" \"should,\" \"could,\" and similar expressions identify forward-looking statements. The Team undertakes no obligation to update forward-looking statements.

ILLUSTRATIVE FIGURES ONLY

All financial figures, vault parameters, fee examples, return scenarios, and token allocation percentages presented in this whitepaper are illustrative only and do not constitute projections, forecasts, or guarantees of future performance. Actual vault sizes, project allocations, equity stakes, investor counts, fee structures, and returns will vary based on each vault\'s specific projects, capital requirements, market conditions, and negotiated terms. The illustrative example in Section 4.3 is a hypothetical scenario designed to demonstrate the vault mechanism and should not be interpreted as a representation of expected returns.

VAULT TOKENS ARE NOT EQUITY

Vault tokens described in this document represent economic rights to vault profits and do NOT confer equity ownership, voting rights, governance rights, or any form of ownership interest in any corporate entity, mining company, or mineral asset. Vault tokens are not shares, securities, units, or partnership interests in any entity. The economic rights associated with vault tokens are limited to the terms described herein and may be subject to change.

HIGH-RISK INVESTMENT

Investment in exploration-stage mining projects involves an exceptionally high degree of risk, including the potential loss of all invested capital. Mining exploration is inherently speculative the majority of exploration projects do not result in economically viable mineral deposits. Research indicates that 75% of mining companies deliver negative shareholder returns over extended periods. Past performance of mining exploration investments, commodity prices, or tokenized asset protocols is not indicative of future results. Prospective participants should only invest funds they can afford to lose entirely.

THIRD-PARTY DATA AND RESEARCH

This whitepaper references data, analysis, and findings from third-party sources including Steve Cole Consulting, McKinsey & Company, Lion Selection Group, and various academic researchers. These references are included for informational context only. The Team has not independently verified all third-party data and makes no representation as to its accuracy or completeness. The inclusion of third-party research does not imply endorsement by or affiliation with any referenced party, nor does it imply endorsement of Minestarters by any referenced party.

REGULATORY STATUS

The Minestarters platform is designed to operate under the Bermuda Digital Asset Business Act (DABA). However, the regulatory classification of vault tokens, the \$MINE protocol token, and Asset-Referenced Tokens may vary across jurisdictions. Vault tokens may be classified as securities, financial instruments, or regulated digital assets in certain jurisdictions. It is the sole responsibility of each prospective participant to determine whether their participation is lawful under the laws of their jurisdiction and to comply with all applicable legal and regulatory requirements, including tax obligations.

Minestarters vault tokens and related digital assets are NOT available to residents or citizens of restricted jurisdictions, including (but not limited to) the United States of America, pending further regulatory guidance. The list of restricted jurisdictions may change without notice.

NO REGULATORY APPROVAL

This whitepaper has not been reviewed, approved, or disapproved by any regulatory authority in any jurisdiction. The publication, distribution, or dissemination of this whitepaper does not imply that applicable laws, regulatory requirements, or rules have been complied with in any jurisdiction.

TECHNOLOGY RISKS

Blockchain technology, smart contracts, and decentralized protocols are experimental technologies subject to risks including but not limited to: smart contract vulnerabilities, hacking, oracle failures, blockchain network congestion, consensus mechanism failures, regulatory changes affecting blockchain operations, and unforeseen technical issues. These risks could result in partial or total loss of funds. Smart contract audits reduce but do not eliminate these risks.

CONFIDENTIALITY

This whitepaper is confidential and is not intended for public distribution. It is intended solely for the recipient and should not be reproduced, redistributed, or passed to any other person, in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the Minestarters Team. Unauthorized distribution may violate applicable securities laws.

GOVERNING VERSION

This whitepaper may be translated into languages other than English. In the event of any inconsistency between the English version and any translated version, the English version shall prevail.

DATE AND VERSION

This document is dated March 2026 (Whitepaper v1.0). The information herein is subject to change without notice. The Team reserves the right to amend, update, or replace this whitepaper at any time. The most current version will be available through official Minestarters channels.

APPENDIX / References & Appendices

15. References & Appendices

Appendix A: \$MINE Token Economics Paper -total supply, allocation schedule, vesting terms, emission curve, staking yield parameters, and governance mechanics.

Appendix B: Smart Contract Technical Specification -function-level documentation, access control matrices, milestone-gated deployment logic, and audit reports.

Appendix C: NAV Tracking Methodology -48+ document type classification, valuation model specifications, oracle integration architecture, and update frequency protocols.

Appendix D: Lassonde Curve Valuation Framework -stage-mapping methodology and development curve integration for vault NAV calculations.

Appendix E: Legal Opinion -Bermuda DABA Compliance.

Appendix F: Legal Opinion- SAC Ring-Fencing Analysis.

Appendix G: AMM Technical Specification - pricing curve design, liquidity incentive parameters, ART minting/redemption mechanics, and POL bond programme structure.

Appendix H: Due Diligence Framework - 8-stage funnel scoring methodology and evaluation criteria.
